title: vepris sansibarensis
intro: Vepris sansibarensis, also known as the Somalian orange bush, is a species of plant in the Rutaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Somalia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its various health benefits such as relieving fever and treating skin conditions. The Somalian orange bush has been used in herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, to treat various health issues, and its essential oils have been extracted for use in traditional medicine. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits and applications of Vepris sansibarensis.
